subject: [PATCH] lockd: pin next file across nlm_inspect_file lock-drop

reviews.author: Jeff Layton
reviews.summary: Raised concern about potential regression in the lockd code, pointing to an external link for further investigation.

reviews.author: Michael Bommarito
reviews.summary: Confirmed the existence of a regression, agreed to fix it and send an updated patch (v2), and mentioned a separate issue with nlmsvc_create_block.
reviews.raw_body:
On Sat, May 23, 2026 at 7:05 AM Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org> wrote:
> Sashiko seems to think there is a regression here. See:
> https://sashiko.dev/#/patchset/20260523014203.2462827-1-michael.bommarito@gmail.com?part=1

Yeah, the predicate check is a real regression.  I'll fix that and send a v2.

The other one  is a separate issue (nlmsvc_create_block).  I never saw
that path fire in my harness, but will send a separate patch if I can
get that to light up.

Thanks,
Mike

2026-05-23.patch_summary: Patch addresses a regression in the lockd code, specifically related to nlm_inspect_file and lock-drop.
